basics.name: Olaya Álvarez-Tuñón
basics.label: Computer Vision & Deep Learning Engineer · Postdoctoral Researcher
basics.summary: Computer Vision and Deep Learning Engineer with extensive expertise in visual odometry and Visual SLAM. My research focuses on machine learning and computer vision within robotics, designing optimal architectures grounded in a geometric understanding of deep learning models. As a roboticist I have hands-on experience with both hardware and software, using ROS (1 and 2) and libraries such as behavior trees.

work.name: ITU Copenhagen & EIVA A/S
work.summary: IFD Project: Deep visual odometry for underwater intervention drones (DeepODO). Development of a deep-learning-based visual SLAM system to enable autonomous underwater robot localization, training neural networks for accurate, real-time camera-based navigation and overcoming unique underwater vision challenges.

work.name: Aarhus University & EIVA A/S
work.summary: EU Project: Reliable AI for Marine Robotics (REMARO).
highlights: Teaching: Autonomous Mobile Robots, Deep Learning, Control Systems.
Research stay at OceanScan-MST: design of behavior trees for AUV mission planning.
Research stay at DFKI: implemented and tested visual SLAM in a testbed with Qualisys ground-truth control.

work.name: RoboticsLab, University Carlos III Madrid
work.summary: EU Project: Long Term Stability Assessment and Monitoring of Flooded Shafts (STAMS).

work.name: R&D Arcelor-Mittal center, Avilés
work.summary: Computer Vision for Automated Defect and Dust Detection on Rolled Steel Plates.

education.area: Visual-based navigation for underwater safety-critical applications
education.studyType: Ph.D. (Marie Curie Scholarship, ETN project REMARO)
highlights: Thesis: Visual-based navigation for underwater-safety critical applications.
Thesis: Visual Localization of an Underwater Robot in Flooded Mine Shafts.
